verses.text: Hearken, Israel, to the statutes I teach you — that ye may live, go in and possess. Add not to the word, neither diminish from it. Your eyes have seen what the LORD did at Baal-peor. Ye that did cleave to the LORD your God are alive every one of you this day. Guidance: A live audience and a fresh grave behind them. Half of the parents died at Peor; their children stand here listening because they held on. Cleaving keeps; drifting kills. Add nothing, take nothing — the word as given is enough.

Keep these statutes, do them: this is your wisdom in the sight of the nations, which shall say, this great nation is a wise and understanding people. For what nation hath God so nigh as the LORD our God is, in all we call upon Him for? Guidance: Obedience is Israel's missionary strategy. The nations watch — and the only thing they need to see is a people whose God is unusually close. The Torah is not a fence to lock the world out; it is a window so the world can see in.

Only take heed lest thou forget what thine eyes have seen. Teach them thy sons and sons' sons. The day thou stoodest before the LORD in Horeb — the mountain burned to the midst of heaven, darkness, clouds, thick darkness. The LORD spake out of the fire — ye heard a voice but saw no similitude. He declared His covenant, the ten commandments, wrote on two tables of stone. Guidance: A mountain on fire, an unseen God, ten words inked in stone. The memory must outlive the parents — teach them to your sons and grandsons. Faith is one generation thick at most, if no one talks.

Take heed — ye saw no manner of similitude when the LORD spake out of the fire. Lest ye make a graven image — male, female, beast. Lest thou lift up thine eyes unto sun, moon, stars, be driven to worship them — which the LORD hath divided unto all nations. The LORD hath taken you out of the iron furnace of Egypt, to be a people of His inheritance. Guidance: He showed Himself with no image so we would refuse images. The sun and stars He gave the nations to admire — but you He pulled out of a furnace to be His own. Israel does not worship what fills the sky; Israel worships the One who hung it.

The LORD was angry with me for your sakes, sware I should not go over Jordan; I die in this land. Take heed lest ye forget the covenant, make a graven image — the LORD thy God is a consuming fire, a jealous God. Guidance: Moses ties his own loss to their warning. He cannot come; you can — don't fumble what cost me my crossing. A consuming fire, a jealous God: the only kind of love worth having is the kind that won't share you.

When ye shall corrupt yourselves — heaven and earth witness, ye shall utterly perish, scattered among the nations. But if from thence thou shalt seek the LORD with all thy heart and soul, thou shalt find Him. In tribulation, in latter days, if thou turn to the LORD — for He is a merciful God; He will not forsake thee, nor forget the covenant of thy fathers. Guidance: Moses sees the future and writes a way home in advance. Even exile is not the end. Heart-and-soul seeking finds Him in any country. Mercy is the spine of the covenant — not a footnote, the spine.

Ask of the days past — did ever a people hear God's voice out of fire and live? Hath God ever taken Him a nation from the midst of another by signs, wonders, a mighty hand? Unto thee it was shewed, that thou mightest know the LORD He is God; there is none else. He loved thy fathers, brought thee out by His presence. Know this day that the LORD He is God in heaven and on earth — there is none else. Guidance: Moses asks them to comparison-shop the gods, and there is no contender. The argument from history: who else has done this? The argument from love: He chose your fathers. There is no other.

Then Moses severed three cities on this side Jordan toward the sunrising, that the slayer might flee thither: Bezer in the wilderness for the Reubenites, Ramoth in Gilead for the Gadites, Golan in Bashan for the Manassites. Guidance: Even mid-sermon, Moses acts. He doesn't wait for the conquest; the east-of-Jordan tribes need their refuges now. The pattern is set before the temple's stones are laid — wherever God's people live, an asylum is built into the geography.

This is the law Moses set before Israel: testimonies, statutes, judgments spoken after they came forth out of Egypt, on this side Jordan in the valley over against Beth-peor, in the land of Sihon and Og whom Israel smote. From Aroer on the Arnon unto mount Hermon, all the eastern plain. Guidance: The address marker — where was Israel when this law was spoken? Right next door to Peor, in territory God just won, with the river ahead. Place matters in God's pedagogy. The law is given where the temptation lurks; the answer was set in the soil before the test arrived.
